title: Relative and absolute URLs

publication.changeSummary: Technology-specific HTML: Semantic and Accessible Web Documents draft for Relative and absolute URLs; independent standards, browser, pedagogy, and accessibility review required. Beginner-quality contract 1.0.0 applied deterministically from existing source-linked draft facts; publication stage and reviewer state are unchanged.

sources.locator: HTML Living Standard; living standard accessed 2026-07; provisions applicable to Relative and absolute URLs
sources.claim: A URL combines scheme, authority, path, query, and fragment, and relative references resolve against the document base URL.

sources.claim: A URL combines scheme, authority, path, query, and fragment, and relative references resolve against the document base URL.

canonicalExplanation.summary: A URL combines scheme, authority, path, query, and fragment, and relative references resolve against the document base URL.

sections.heading: Start here
sections.body: Start with this case: From /courses/html/index.html, the relative link ../css/ resolves to /courses/css/ under the current base. Your goal is to construct and debug links across a multi-folder site and within a page. Work through the example one step at a time, then use the boundary to check your answer.

sections.heading: Before you begin
sections.body: Review these declared prerequisites first: Sections and landmarks; pf tools files; Standards-mode document skeleton. If one is unfamiliar, revisit it before continuing.

sections.heading: Technology rule
sections.body: A URL combines scheme, authority, path, query, and fragment, and relative references resolve against the document base URL. Apply this rule to the actual markup, stylesheet, JavaScript, or component state rather than memorizing its name.

sections.heading: Boundary and browser reality
sections.body: Encoding, trailing slashes, base elements, origin rules, and deployment subpaths can change resolution. This draft therefore rejects: Relative URLs always resolve from the website root.

includes: Resolve paths, fragments, query strings and schemes from a document base.
Construct and debug links across a multi-folder site and within a page.
excludes: Encoding, trailing slashes, base elements, origin rules, and deployment subpaths can change resolution.

outcomes: Construct and debug links across a multi-folder site and within a page.

examples.title: Relative and absolute URLs: concrete case
examples.situation: From /courses/html/index.html, the relative link ../css/ resolves to /courses/css/ under the current base.
walkthrough: Step 1 — Inspect the exact artifact and starting state: From /courses/html/index.html, the relative link ../css/ resolves to /courses/css/ under the current base.
Step 2 — Use the core rule above to predict one observable result.
Step 3 — Verify the correction: Resolve relative URLs against the document base URL, not automatically against the origin root.
examples.result: The result follows because Resolve relative URLs against the document base URL, not automatically against the origin root.

examples.title: Relative and absolute URLs: boundary case
examples.situation: Encoding, trailing slashes, base elements, origin rules, and deployment subpaths can change resolution.

Step 2 — Use the core rule above to predict one observable result.
Step 3 — Reject the misconception: Relative URLs always resolve from the website root.
examples.result: The boundary requires this correction: Resolve relative URLs against the document base URL, not automatically against the origin root.
